paragraphs.text: As a gamer, but also as a QV Tester working at a video game company, I am Afraid that the games I Bought or those I worked on, to make sure that they reach the players in the best conditions, one day they will disappear, along with the hours of work put in and the money spent to buy them. I would not want a game that I contributed to disappear after a while, I put effort and soul into it. I would like there to be a legal and safe solution to preserve these games after the technical support ends. I understand perfectly what the technical and financial difficulties are to provide support for a game indefinitely, but there are already technical solutions that developers can implement if the company’s management allows them to do so, to keep the game in a functional state. Sides the problem of losing the game that I paid for and invested hours to play, there is another problem that is caused by these practices of Destroying video games, namely piracy. ONCE a game is no longer supported and distributed, you have Nowhere to legally purchase it, and the only solution is the sites that distribute Pirated games. A solution that is not always safe. They can contain viruses, or solutions that are too complicated to make the game run. A very good part of the torrent sites offering perfectly safe games for free, without viruses or malicious data collection, but some sites have scams that can steal user data and more. A piece of music or a movie can still be purchased legally in perfect safety, on the other hand, with video games it is almost impossible, except for games from the GOG (Good Old Games) library and other similar platforms. But the number of games that can still be legally purchased after they are no longer supported is Extremely small compared to the number of games that are lost, and this number has started to grow at an Alarming rate in the last 10-15 years, since companies start implementing features that requires a permanent connection to the internet without a plan for the end of life of the game. Many games having these components unjustifiably, even for games that also have a single player component. But these platforms are not safe either, not even a platform like GOG can guarantee that it can keep the game on the platform indefinitely, once the publisher comes and asks them to take down a certain game, they are forced to comply. Another problem that is not only in video games, is the license and TOS. Sometimes you can only read them after you have Bought the product, and after you have Bought the product and you do not agree with what is written in the License Agreement and TOS, you can no longer have access to that product, not even a trial period to decide if it is worth accepting those agreements or not. These practices must be regulated. There are many good games, but also very useful software, but which have agreements and licenses that are anti-consumer, a practice through which they primarily Mock those software, but also the users. Games are a form of art, which encapsulates the culture of the period they are part of (music, pop references, Illustrations, design. etc.) It is a pop culture that must be preserved in perfect legality and safety. If we can preserve music, movies and series, why can we also preserve video games, but also any software, whether in physical or digital format.
